sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐκκλίνω

- Transliteration: Ekklino

- Phonetic: ek-klee'-no

- Definition:

1. to turn aside, deviate (from the right way and course)

2. to turn (one's self) away, to turn away from, keep aloof from one's society

3. to shun one

- Origin: from G1537 and G2827

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1537 and G2827; to deviate that is (absolutely) to shun (literally or figuratively) or (relatively) to decline (from piety): - avoid eschew go out of the way.
